feedback.text: I support the 'Stop Killing Games' movement. I would like to request that the European parliament looks into stopping the behavior from publishers of video games who stop support of video games without providing an offline alternative. When the consumer buys a game, it should be their property and not just some license without an expiration date. A solution to this problem would be to oblige publishers to provide an offline installer of the game that does not need their servers or any other online service to install and run the game.
